claim: LLMs are stateless and lack business knowledge, making RAG (vector database retrieval of company context) essential to ground LLM responses in a company's actual data rather than generic internet knowledge.
actionable: Deploy vector databases to retrieve context specific to your business domain before passing queries to LLMs, ensuring responses reflect your company's data not generic training knowledge.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF: Emma McGrattan: Engineering the Context Layer

claim: Regulatory pressures, sub-millisecond latency requirements, and data gravity (hundreds of internal data sources) make cloud-only AI architectures insufficient for enterprises.
actionable: Design AI deployments assuming hybrid topology from the start: cloud for elastic scale, on-premises for regulated data, edge for sub-millisecond decisions, with intelligent query routing.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF: Emma McGrattan: Engineering the Context Layer

claim: Cloud introduces 20-200ms latency and egress costs; on-premises requires upfront infrastructure investment but necessary for data sovereignty; edge enables millisecond decisions but has limited compute and memory.
actionable: Choose topology per workload: cloud for non-sensitive data with relaxed latency, on-premises for regulated data, edge for real-time decisions; implement intelligent routing to send queries to appropriate tier.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF: Emma McGrattan: Engineering the Context Layer

claim: Multimodal retrieval (audio, image, time-series) will become expected within 12-18 months, and governance-aware retrieval built into indexes (not applied after fact) is the future.
actionable: Plan RAG infrastructure to support multimodal data types and embed governance rules into retrieval indexes rather than applying access control post-retrieval.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF: Emma McGrattan: Engineering the Context Layer
